column1: She is the finest of cooks (outside of myself, that is).  But her fine food is as dust in my mouth, for I am but a beggar.
column2: 「她是最好的廚師(當然除了我自己啦)。不過她做的美食對我來說就像塵埃一樣,因為我已經淪落到乞丐的地步。」

column1: I had treasures carefully brought from Shapeir with me.  I even had a Magic Carpet with me!
column2: 「我帶來了從沙佩爾小心翼翼運來的珍寶。我甚至還帶來了一條魔法毯!」

column1: The Wizard was very short, and he giggled most of the time.  I couldn't understand what he was saying.
column2: 「那位巫師個子很矮,大部分時間都在咯咯笑。我根本聽不懂他在說什麼。」

column1: What did Abdulla do to you that you should do him in?
column2: 你對阿布杜拉做了什麼,以至於他會想害你?

column1: The merchant is a rather fat man with a small moustache and beard.  He wears a turban.
column2: 商人是個相當肥胖的傢伙,長著小鬍子和鬍鬚。他戴著頭巾。

column1: The merchant gratefully accepts your food ration, and consumes it in record time.
column2: 商人感激地接受你的食物配給,並以創紀錄的速度吃完了它。

column1: The merchant gratefully accepts your offer.
column2: 商人感激地接受了你的提議。

column1: Abdulla Doo consumes his meal in record time.
column2: 阿布杜拉·嘟以創紀錄的速度吃完了他的飯菜。

column1: Gladly will I allow you to buy me a meal, O Prince among Heroes.
column2: 「我很樂意讓你請我吃飯,英雄中的王子啊。」

column1: Your offer is gracious, but keep your coins.  Abdulla Doo is no beggar.
column2: 「你的提議雖然慷慨,但把硬幣留著吧。阿布杜拉·嘟不是乞丐。」

column1: Your lips say yes, but your purse says no.
column2: 你嘴上說好,但你的錢包卻說不行。

column1: Shema, prepare for me the feast of six houris.  I am willing to wait.
column2: 「席瑪啊,給我準備六仙女的盛宴。我願意等。」

column1: Shema, prepare for me the thrice brewed tea of the Southern Seas.  I am willing to wait.
column2: 「席瑪啊,給我泡南海的三次釀造茶。我願意等。」

column1: Abdulla already has one expensive-sounding order pending.
column2: 阿布杜拉已經有一個聽起來很貴的訂單待處理。

column1: Grateful is my parched throat, noble Hero.  Your generosity is unequalled in seven kingdoms.
column2: 「我乾渴的喉嚨感到感激,高尚的英雄。你的慷慨在七個王國裡無人能及。」

column1: Carpet
column2: 地毯

column1: Commands
column2: 命令

column1: Friends
column2: 朋友

column1: Merchant
column2: 商人

column1: Robbery
column2: 搶劫

column1: Shameen
column2: 沙敏

column1: Shapeir
column2: 沙佩爾

column1: Shema
column2: 席瑪

column1: Wealth
column2: 財富

column1: Wizard
column2: 巫師

column1: The bench is finely made, in contrast to the rough-hewn tables.
column2: 長椅做得很精緻,與粗糙的桌子形成了對比。

column1: The odor of cooking food wafts through the curtains.
column2: 烹飪食物的香氣飄過窗簾。

column1: The warmth of the fire feels good on such a chilly day.  The proprietor of the inn apparently thinks so, too.  Cats always seem to gravitate to a warm hearth.
column2: 在這樣寒冷的日子裡,火的溫暖令人感到舒服。客棧老闆顯然也這麼認為。貓咪似乎總是喜歡靠近溫暖的爐火。

column1: A well-fed fire purrs happily in the fireplace.
column2: 一團燒得很旺的火在壁爐裡快樂地「呼呼」作聲。

column1: The cheery firelight reflects off the freshly waxed floor.
column2: 歡樂的火光映照在剛剛打蠟的地板上。

column1: You practically licked the plate clean.  That was delicious.
column2: 你幾乎把盤子舔得乾乾淨淨。那真是美味!

column1: The food looks unusual, but smells surprisingly good.
column2: 食物看起來很奇怪,但聞起來出奇地好。

column1: You look closely and read:  "Genie's lamp for rent.  Inquire in 'Quest for Glory 2:  Trial By Fire' for details."  Oh, it's just another cheap plug.
column2: 你仔細看,讀到:「精靈神燈出租。詳情請參閱《英雄傳奇2:烈火之試》。」唔,又是另一個廉價的廣告。

column1: Ouch.
column2: 哎喲!

column1: This is the idiot half-brother candle.  (It's only half-bright.)
column2: 這是那個笨蛋半兄弟的蠟燭。(它只有一半亮。)

column1: This authentic Polynesian mask is far, far from home.
column2: 這個道地的波利尼西亞面具離家太遠太遠了。

column1: This well-used ale tankard has seen fuller days.
column2: 這個用舊了的啤酒杯曾經盛滿過。

column1: That well-stuffed pillow looks more comfortable than these hard wooden benches.
column2: 那個鼓鼓囊囊的枕頭看起來比這些硬木板凳舒服多了。

column1: Collection of designer plates loaned to the Hero's Inn by Mary Meister.
column2: 由瑪麗·麥斯特先生借給英雄客棧的設計師盤子合集。

column1: One of Hiram Plugmeister's double-barreled spitoons.
column2: 海勒姆·普勒格邁斯特先生的雙管痰盂之一。

column1: The food tastes surprisingly good, and the drink is delicious.
column2: 食物的味道出奇地好,飲料也很美味。

column1: The beverage goes down smoothly and well.
column2: 飲料順利爽快地下了肚。

column1: Hey!  Stop that!  What do you think this is: some kind of biker bar?
column2: 嘿!別鬧了!你以為這是什麼?機車酒吧嗎?

column1: You take a seat at the table nearest the fire.
column2: 你在最靠近火爐的桌子前坐了下來。

column1: You'd better check your skill list.  Ornamental carving isn't on there.
column2: 你最好檢查一下你的技能列表。裡面沒有裝飾雕刻。

column1: You see a sign that says, "Please don't touch."  Besides, Shameen is watching you.
column2: 你看到一個牌子上寫著「請勿觸碰」。另外,沙敏正在看著你。

column1: That wood never work; the table is too board and logy to respond.  "Planks anyway," you tell it.
column2: 那木頭不會有反應;這桌子太硬、太遲鈍了。「算了,就是塊木板。」你這樣告訴它。

column1: This looks like an alpine inn, complete with dishes on the wall and heavy beams on the ceiling.  It is pleasantly warm, and the aroma of strange spices fills the air.
column2: 這看起來像是一家山區客棧,牆上掛著盤子,天花板上有粗重的樑柱。裡面溫暖宜人,奇異香料的香氣充滿了空間。

column1: You must pay the innkeeper for a room.
column2: 你必須為房間付錢給客棧老闆。

column1: Finish your drink.
column2: 喝完你的飲料。

column1: Eat your meal first.
column2: 先吃你的飯菜。

column1: You should wait for Shema to bring you what you ordered.
column2: 你應該等席瑪把你點的東西帶過來。

column1: This is the brighter candle.
column2: 這是那個比較亮的蠟燭。

column1: Welcome!  Welcome, traveler, to the Hero's Tale Inn!  I am Shameen, at your service.  May you find what you seek here.
column2: 「歡迎!歡迎,旅人,來到英雄客棧!我叫沙敏,為你效勞。希望你能在這裡找到你要找的東西。」

column1: May you dream of all the rewards you deserve.
column2: 「願你夢到所有你應得的獎勵。」

column1: You thank Shameen and pay him 5 silvers for the room.
column2: 你感謝沙敏,為房間付了他 5 枚銀幣。

column1: He is a merchant from my homeland of Shapeir, a dear friend and guest here.
column2: 「他是來自我的故鄉沙佩爾的商人,我的好友和客人。」

column1: I know little about them except they must be wealthy now.
column2: 「我對他們知之甚少,只知道他們現在一定很有錢。」

column1: In my homeland, a caravan is the only way to travel from city to city.
column2: 「在我的故鄉,駝隊是城市間唯一的交通方式。」

column1: Very good food, very good drink, finest in town.  Sit, rest, you will be served by my Shema.
column2: 「食物很好,飲料也很好,鎮上最好的。坐著,休息一下,我的席瑪會招待你。」

column1: We live far to the south in the desert lands of Shapeir. Someday we may yet return there.
column2: 「我們住在遙遠的南方,沙佩爾的沙漠地帶。也許有一天我們能回到那裡。」

column1: This is the Hero's Tale Inn, where Shema and I will be delighted to serve you.
column2: 「這是英雄客棧,席瑪和我都很樂意為你效勞。」

column1: The softest and safest of beds, the richest and finest of foods awaits you. You have but to command.
column2: 「最柔軟、最安全的床,最豐富、最精緻的食物等著你。你只需下令。」

column1: We are a race of desert dwellers and wanderers of the endless sands.
column2: 「我們是沙漠居民的種族,是無盡沙漠的漂泊者。」

column1: Poor Abdulla sits and tries to forget his loss in food and drink.  I think he will not be sorry if you join him at his table.
column2: 「可憐的阿布杜拉坐著,試著用食物和飲料來忘卻他的損失。我想如果你與他一起坐,他不會反對。」

column1: My friend, Abdulla, is recovering from his robbery last week. He should be here around suppertime.
column2: 「我的朋友阿布杜拉正在從上週的搶劫中恢復。他應該會在晚餐時間前後到達。」

column1: I am Shameen, a poor unfortunate, forced to seek a fortune in this cold land far away from my homeland and my Katta people.
column2: 「我叫沙敏,一個可憐的不幸者,被迫在這個寒冷的地方遠離我的故鄉和卡塔族人去尋求財富。」

column1: There is no end to the food that that one can eat!
column2: 「那傢伙能吃的食物根本沒有盡頭!」

column1: Alas, the brigands stole all of Abdulla's wealth and mine own when they robbed his caravan.  Now I will be forced to stay in this wet, cold land.
column2: 「唉呀,那些山賊搶劫了他的駝隊時,連阿布杜拉和我的所有財富都偷走了。現在我被迫留在這個濕冷的地方。」

column1: Very nice room, very soft beds -- finest in town.  Only five silvers a night.
column2: 「房間很不錯,床也很軟——鎮上最好的。一晚只要 5 枚銀幣。」

column1: A land of golden sand and tall mountains, a city of tall buildings and winding streets!
column2: 「一片黃金沙漠和高大的山脈,一座有著高聳建築和蜿蜒街道的城市!」

column1: A place where Katta can bask in the sun as they sell things in the bazaar.
column2: 「一個卡塔族人可以享受陽光,在市集裡販賣商品的地方。」

column1: It is our homeland, and a place we long to be.
column2: 「這是我們的故鄉,也是我們渴望回到的地方。」

column1: Shema is my beloved, and she can cook and keep an Inn as few others.
column2: 「席瑪是我的摯愛,她的烹飪和經營客棧的本領無人能及。」

column1: When she dances, there is true magic.  If we ever return to Shapeir, then she will again dance.
column2: 「她跳舞時有著真正的魔法。如果我們有朝一日能回到沙佩爾,她將再次跳舞。」

column1: He comes in with his wife sometimes for food.  He is very wise and generous.
column2: 「他有時會和他的妻子一起來吃飯。他非常聰慧且慷慨。」

column1: This is a cold land for a Katta.
column2: 「對卡塔族人來說,這是個寒冷的地方。」

column1: Spielburg is a sad place for an Innkeeper, for few travelers visit because of the brigands.
column2: 「史畢柏格對於客棧老闆來說是個悲哀的地方,因為很少有旅人來訪,都是因為那些山賊。」

column1: I hoped to return to my homeland with the profits Abdulla, Shema, and I would make from the caravan.  Now, I fear, we will remain here unless someone deals with the brigands and returns our stolen trade items.
column2: 「我原本希望能用駝隊生意的利潤和阿布杜拉、席瑪一起回到故鄉。現在,我擔心,除非有人解決掉那些山賊並歸還我們被盜的商品,否則我們會留在這裡。」

column1: You think better of it; those claws look sharp.
column2: 你還是打消了這個念頭;那些爪子看起來很尖銳。

column1: You see a small, cat-like creature known as a Katta.  You've heard that Kattas are common in the southern deserts.
column2: 你看到一隻小貓咪似的生物,叫做卡塔族。你聽說過卡塔族人在南方沙漠中很常見。

column1: Because your silver is too low, you must find other accomodations.  Perhaps you can find work somewhere.
column2: 因為你的銀幣不夠,你必須尋找其他住處。也許你能在某個地方找到工作。

column1: It's too early to go to bed; you don't feel tired enough to sleep.
column2: 現在去睡覺太早了;你還不夠累,睡不著。

column1: I dare not offend my wife by letting you rent a room before she has finished serving you.
column2: 「我不敢在我老婆還沒有招待你的時候就讓你租房間而惹惱她。」

column1: You are sure that you would not be able to sleep, because you are so hungry.
column2: 你確信你無法入睡,因為你實在太餓了。

column1: Very good food, very good drink, finest in town.  Sit, rest, you will be served by my Shema.
column2: 「食物很好,飲料也很好,鎮上最好的。坐著,休息一下,我的席瑪會招待你。」

column1: Abdulla
column2: 阿布杜拉

column1: Food
column2: 食物

column1: Homeland
column2: 故鄉

column1: Inn
column2: 客棧

column1: Katta
column2: 卡塔族

column1: Room
column2: 房間

column1: Spielburg
column2: 史畢柏格

column1: I bring you that which you ordered.  May it please and satisfy you.
column2: 「我為你帶來了你所點的東西。希望它能取悅並滿足你。」

column1: I am Shema.  Allow me to serve you, Wanderer from Afar.  Do you wish food or drink?
column2: 「我叫席瑪。讓我為你效勞,遠方的流浪者。你要食物還是飲料?」

column1: Permit me to clear the table for you.
column2: 「請讓我為你收拾桌子。」

column1: Abdulla, too, is from the land of Shapeir.
column2: 「阿布杜拉也是來自沙佩爾。」

column1: It is true I love to dance, but my soul can not fly so far from home.
column2: 「我確實喜歡跳舞,但我的靈魂無法飛得離家這麼遠。」

column1: We have rich black coffee, freshly ground and brewed as only my people do. Such coffee will bring you to your full alertness.  It is but a silver.
column2: 「我們有濃郁的黑咖啡,剛現磨現泡的,只有我們卡塔族人才這樣做。這樣的咖啡能讓你保持完全清醒。只要一枚銀幣。」

column1: We have very good stew of newborn lamb along with honey chicken for a mere three silvers.  Coffee is included in the price of the meal.
column2: 「我們有很好的羊羔燉菜和蜂蜜雞,只要 3 枚銀幣。咖啡已經包括在飯菜的價格裡了。」

column1: Our homeland is far to the south.  It is called Shapeir.
column2: 「我們的故鄉遠在南方。叫沙佩爾。」

column1: Katta are a quiet, gentle people.
column2: 「卡塔族人是個安靜、溫柔的民族。」

column1: Abdulla will always be our guest and friend.
column2: 「阿布杜拉永遠是我們的客人和朋友。」

column1: I am Shema, which means in the words of the Katta, 'She of the Dance.'
column2: 「我叫席瑪,用卡塔族的語言,意思是『舞者』。」

column1: It is Shameen who is the speaker for the house, for I have much work to do to serve you.
column2: 「家裡的發言人是沙敏,因為我還有很多工作要做來招待你。」

column1: If you wish to know more about such things, then you should speak to him.
column2: 「如果你想知道更多這方面的事,你應該和他說。」

column1: Abdulla will be honored to tell you of that.
column2: 「阿布杜拉會很樂意為你講述那些。」

column1: You have but to give Shameen five silvers, and you may have a bed to dream upon.
column2: 「你只需給沙敏 5 枚銀幣,就能有張床來躺著做夢。」

column1: He is my soulmate.
column2: 「他是我的靈魂伴侶。」

column1: Shapeir is where we Katta come from.  It is warm and glowing and the sun is always bright.
column2: 「沙佩爾是我們卡塔族人的故鄉。那裡溫暖閃亮,陽光永遠燦爛。」

column1: Her claws look too sharp for you to risk her (or her husband's) wrath.
column2: 她的爪子看起來太尖了,你不敢冒她(或她丈夫)的怒火。

column1: You see a rather attractive female Katta wearing her native costume.
column2: 你看到一隻長得相當漂亮的母卡塔族人,穿著她的民族服裝。

column1: One more cup of coffee and you'll be up all night.  Better quit now.
column2: 再來一杯咖啡你就整晚睡不著了。還是現在戒了吧。

column1: You caffeine addicts are all alike.
column2: 你們這些咖啡因上癮的傢伙都一樣。

column1: There's no need.  You're not hungry.
column2: 沒必要。你不餓。

column1: You pay Shema for a meal.
column2: 你付錢給席瑪買飯菜。

column1: You decide not to buy that.
column2: 你決定不買那個了。

column1: You are ravenous and immediately pay for a meal.
column2: 你非常飢餓,立刻付錢買飯菜。

column1: Dance
column2: 舞蹈

column1: Drink
column2: 飲料

column1: Questions
column2: 問題

column1: That door leads to the sleeping rooms.  If you desire a room, it will cost a mere 5 silvers.
column2: 「那扇門通向睡眠室。如果你想要一間房間,只要 5 枚銀幣。」

column1: A door to some other room.  Perhaps the proprietor can tell you what's in there.
column2: 通往其他房間的門。也許客棧老闆能告訴你裡面有什麼。

column1: You are too polite to enter the kitchen uninvited.
column2: 你太禮貌了,不敢未經邀請進入廚房。

column1: The odor of cooking wafts through the curtains.
column2: 烹飪食物的香氣飄過窗簾。

column1: The table feels rough to the touch, but very solid.
column2: 桌子摸起來很粗糙,但非常牢固。

column1: The tables look like they have been rough-cut from some of the local pines.
column2: 這些桌子看起來像是用當地松木粗糙地切割而成的。

column1: The coffee is black as night, hot as hell, and sweet as a lover's kiss.
column2: 咖啡黑如夜晚,熱如地獄,甜如戀人之吻。

column1: You see an assortment of tools used to maintain the fire.
column2: 你看到各種用來維護火爐的工具。

column1: The sleep heals and refreshes you.
column2: 睡眠治癒並恢復了你的精力。
